i18n Language Switcher Module
nodes.description: Self-contained IIFE providing multilingual support (EN/FR/ES) via curated dictionary + Google Translate fallback

nodes.label: Site JS Module
nodes.description: Self-contained IIFE providing mobile nav, accordion, blog toggle, image comparison slider, smooth scroll, and console easter egg

nodes.label: CSS Audit Script
nodes.description: Compares Tailwind CSS classes defined in CSS vs used in HTML to find missing and unused classes

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md Project Configuration
nodes.description: Project-level Claude Code instructions including graphify integration rules

nodes.label: Graph Report (2026-04-11)
nodes.description: Knowledge graph report with 115 nodes, 108 edges, 23 communities for the redesign-2026 project

nodes.label: robots.txt
nodes.description: Search engine crawler configuration allowing all agents and pointing to sitemap

nodes.label: Google Translate Integration
nodes.description: External dependency on Google Translate API for full-page translation of content not covered by curated dictionary

nodes.label: Curated Translation Dictionary
nodes.description: Hand-written FR/ES translations for ~40 key UI strings (nav, footer, CTAs) providing instant high-quality translations

nodes.label: Language Persistence (localStorage)
nodes.description: Language preference persisted via localStorage key ooc-lang and Google Translate cookie googtrans

nodes.label: Nav Overflow Guard
nodes.description: Three-stage responsive nav: full logo -> compact icon logo -> hamburger menu, triggered by content overflow measurement

nodes.label: Image Comparison Slider Widget
nodes.description: Before/after image comparison with pointer drag, dark/light pill label switching

nodes.label: Console Easter Egg (Curious Coder)
nodes.description: ASCII art logo and sustainability message logged to browser console for developers inspecting the site

nodes.label: Tailwind Minified CSS
nodes.description: Compiled Tailwind CSS file audited by the CSS audit script

nodes.label: Custom/JS-Injected Classes Allowlist
nodes.description: Set of ~35 class names injected by JS (nav dropdowns, impact stats, ticker, footer) excluded from missing-class reports

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Website
nodes.description: The oasisofchange.com website, the product of this redesign-2026 project

nodes.label: Sitemap XML
nodes.description: XML sitemap referenced by robots.txt at https://oasisofchange.com/sitemap.xml

nodes.label: Rationale: Dictionary-First Translation Strategy
nodes.description: Curated dictionary applied first for instant, high-quality nav/footer translations without flash of untranslated content, with Google Translate as fallback for remaining page content

nodes.label: Rationale: Three-Stage Nav Collapse
nodes.description: Nav uses three progressive stages (full logo, icon-only logo, hamburger) to maximize horizontal space before collapsing, avoiding premature hamburger on medium viewports

nodes.label: Rationale: Hide Google Translate Chrome
nodes.description: Google Translate default banner, tooltip, and highlight UI is hidden via injected CSS to maintain the site clean design while still using GT for translation

nodes.label: Rationale: Custom Class Exclusion in Audit
nodes.description: JS-injected classes are excluded from the missing-class report because they are styled in site.css or inline, not in the Tailwind build - prevents false positives in the audit

nodes.label: Prefers Reduced Motion Support
nodes.description: Mobile nav respects prefers-reduced-motion media query, skipping slide/fade animations for accessibility

Denman Case Study Preview â€” Abstract Ink/Paint Art
nodes.description_extracted: Abstract fluid art image featuring swirling teal/aqua and orange ink or paint droplets on a white background. No people visible. Evokes emotional or therapeutic themes through color and fluid motion.
nodes.description_inferred: Used as a mood or hero image for the Denman case study on the Oasis of Change website. The abstract, fluid aesthetic likely represents emotional transformation or a therapeutic/creative journey associated with the Denman client or project.

nodes.label: Jordan Mittler â€” Portrait Photo
nodes.description_extracted: Headshot/portrait of a young man, approximately late teens to mid-twenties, with short dark hair and a broad smile. He is wearing a light blue plaid button-down shirt (Polo Ralph Lauren logo visible). Arms are crossed. Background shows a coastal scene with rocks, blue water, and distant harbor or marina at dusk/early evening.
nodes.description_inferred: This is Jordan Mittler, identified by the filename. Likely a client or subject of an Oasis of Change case study. The outdoor coastal setting and confident posture project positivity and wellbeing, consistent with a recovery or growth narrative.

nodes.label: Mittler Case Study Preview â€” Couple at Vineyard/Countryside
nodes.description_extracted: Two people â€” an older woman wearing a wide-brimmed straw hat with red flower and a striped top, and an older man in a white cap and plaid shirt â€” seated side by side on a stone bench or ledge, viewed from behind. They are looking out at a lush green vineyard or orchard with rolling tree-covered hills in the background. The setting is peaceful, summery, and rural.
nodes.description_inferred: Used as the hero/preview image for the Mittler case study. The two figures are likely the parents or family members of Jordan Mittler, or represent a couple whose story is told in the case study. The vineyard and contemplative pose evoke themes of reflection, peace, family connection, and life after hardship â€” consistent with Oasis of Change's therapeutic mission.

nodes.label: Denman Case Study
nodes.description_extracted: Case study entry on Oasis of Change website associated with a client or subject named Denman. Represented visually by an abstract fluid/ink art image.
nodes.description_inferred: The abstract art style may reflect the nature of the Denman case â€” possibly a creative, artistic, or emotionally complex therapeutic journey.

nodes.label: Mittler Case Study
nodes.description_extracted: Case study entry on Oasis of Change website associated with the Mittler family or Jordan Mittler. Represented visually by a couple seated at a vineyard.
nodes.description_inferred: The Mittler case study likely involves family dynamics, recovery, or personal transformation. The peaceful vineyard image conveys resolution and tranquility.

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Technology Hero Background
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A young male student (likely high school or college age) wearing a blue polo shirt sits at a desk giving a thumbs-up gesture toward an iMac computer screen showing a video call with a grid of participants (Zoom/video conference interface). The room is casual and personal, with sports memorabilia and a bulletin board visible in the background. INFERRED: This image represents a Mittler Senior Technology tutor conducting an online technology class with senior citizens. The energetic thumbs-up gesture conveys enthusiasm and positive engagement. The Apple iMac and informal bedroom/study setting reinforce the 'run by high school and college students' brand narrative.

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Technology Website After Redesign
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A full-page screenshot of the redesigned Mittler Senior Technology website. The hero section features bold white headline text 'Technology Education for Senior Citizens' over a blurred background image (the hero-bg photo of a young tutor at a computer). Below the headline is a short tagline and two prominent CTA buttons labeled 'For Seniors' and 'For Organizations' in green. Below the hero are four stat tiles showing '3,500+ Seniors Impacted', '450+ Classes Taught', '150+ Teens Inspired', '10+ Countries Reached'. A 'Classes & Services' section heading appears below. The navigation bar is clean with white background, green logo, and right-aligned nav links including a 'Sponsor a Class' CTA button in green. INFERRED: The redesign emphasizes social proof, clarity, and audience segmentation (seniors vs. organizations). The green color palette aligns with the existing brand. The layout is modern, spacious, and conversion-focused with statistics driving credibility.
nodes.visual_tone: modern, clean, credibility-focused, green brand palette

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Technology Website Before Redesign
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A full-page screenshot of the original Mittler Senior Technology website before redesign. The top section contains a dark navy banner with large bold yellow text reading 'TECH TIP TUESDAY' alongside white body text describing a weekly tech tip clip series distributed via WhatsApp, email, YouTube, and social media. Below is a two-column layout with sections titled 'Private Technology Assistance' and 'Group Classes', both using dense paragraph text with 'click here' hyperlinks. A left sidebar contains navigation links (Home, Classes & Services, Our Story, Our Mission, Contact & Tutorials, Testimonials, In The News, Sample Classes). Bottom row shows three small thumbnail images. The overall visual design is outdated, text-heavy, low contrast, and lacks a clear visual hierarchy. INFERRED: The old site predates modern UI conventions, with no prominent hero, no stat-driven social proof, no audience segmentation, and an overloaded navigation structure. The 'click here' anchor text is a known accessibility anti-pattern.
nodes.visual_tone: dated, cluttered, low visual hierarchy, text-heavy

nodes.label: Nature Sustainability Case Study Hero
nodes.description: EXTRACTED: A wide-angle photograph of a solar panel array (photovoltaic field) extending diagonally across the lower half of the frame under a vivid bright blue sky with wispy white cirrus clouds. The panels are mounted on metal support structures and appear to be in an open rural or semi-arid landscape. INFERRED: This image is used as the hero/banner background for a sustainability or clean energy case study. The expansive sky and solar panels visually communicate themes of renewable energy, environmental sustainability, clean technology, and large-scale impact. The bright blue and silver tones project optimism and forward-thinking.

nodes.label: Mittler Senior Tech Before/After Redesign Comparison
nodes.description: INFERRED: The before/after pair documents a significant UX and visual redesign. Before: sidebar-nav, dark announcement banner, dense prose, no social proof, low hierarchy, accessibility issues. After: sticky top nav, full-bleed hero with photo overlay, dual audience CTAs, stat tiles for credibility, modern spacing and typography. The redesign dramatically improves conversion potential and accessibility compliance.

nodes.label: Android Chrome Icon 192x192 â€“ Oasis of Change Logo
nodes.description: PWA Android Chrome icon at 192x192px. Circular design featuring two green leaves rising upward from the center (bright green ~#3aaa35 and darker green ~#2d8a28), flanked by curved green arc strokes on left and right sides, set above a blue water/wave shape split into dark navy blue (~#2c4fa3) and lighter sky blue (~#4a90d9). White background. Represents nature, growth, and water â€” consistent with an environmental/sustainability brand identity.
nodes.icon_purpose: PWA Android Chrome home screen icon
nodes.motif: two leaves with arc strokes above a blue wave/water shape

nodes.label: Android Chrome Icon 512x512 â€“ Oasis of Change Logo
nodes.description: PWA Android Chrome splash/maskable icon at 512x512px. Same circular design as 192x192 but at higher resolution: two green leaves (bright and dark green) rising from center, flanked by curved green arc strokes, above a blue dual-tone water wave (dark navy + lighter blue). White background. Used for PWA splash screens and high-DPI displays.
nodes.icon_purpose: PWA Android Chrome splash screen / high-res app icon
nodes.motif: two leaves with arc strokes above a blue wave/water shape

nodes.label: Apple Touch Icon â€“ Oasis of Change Logo
nodes.description: Apple Touch Icon (typically 180x180px) for iOS home screen bookmarks. Same circular logo: two green leaves, curved green arc strokes, blue dual-tone water wave base. White background. Used when iOS users add the site to their home screen.
nodes.icon_purpose: iOS Apple Touch Icon for home screen bookmark
nodes.motif: two leaves with arc strokes above a blue wave/water shape

nodes.label: Favicon 16x16 â€“ Oasis of Change
nodes.description: Standard browser favicon at 16x16px. Tiny rendition of the circular Oasis of Change logo: green leaf shapes visible above blue water base. At this scale detail is minimal but green and blue color identity is preserved.
nodes.motif: simplified green leaves above blue wave

nodes.label: Favicon 32x32 â€“ Oasis of Change
nodes.description: Standard browser favicon at 32x32px. Clearer rendition of the circular Oasis of Change logo at slightly higher resolution: two green leaves with arc strokes, blue dual-tone water wave base. Used in browser tabs on higher-DPI displays and bookmark bars.
nodes.motif: two leaves with arc strokes above blue wave

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Brand Logo
nodes.description: The Oasis of Change brand logo concept: a circular icon combining green leaf growth motif (nature, sustainability, environmental action) with a blue water/wave shape (water, oasis, renewal). The dual-tone blue base and rising leaves visually encode the brand name 'Oasis of Change'.

nodes.label: PWA Icon Set â€“ Oasis of Change
nodes.description: The complete set of Progressive Web App (PWA) and browser icons for the Oasis of Change website, including Android Chrome icons (192x192, 512x512), Apple Touch Icon (180x180), and standard favicons (16x16, 32x32). All icons share the same circular green-and-blue logo design.

nodes.label: Tree Transparency Report Dashboard
nodes.description: UI dashboard titled 'Tree Transparency Report' showing verified planting data, partner contributions, and climate outcomes. Displays 17,001 trees planted, 1.7% progress toward 1M trees by 2030, and metrics: 8,663 families, 7,338 hectares, 32 countries, 6 continents, 96 species, 160 partners, 545,831+ CO2 offset. Header shows Oasis of Change, Inc. branding in dark green.

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Inc. Dashboard Branding
nodes.description: Dark green navigation bar branded 'Oasis of Change, Inc.' with tabs: Dashboard, Impact Report, Tree Species, Gallery, News. Subheader shows '2025-2026 FY Planting Complete: 1,444 trees in 14 countries | View Impact'.

nodes.label: CO2 Offset Metric (545,831+)
nodes.description: Dashboard stat showing 545,831+ CO2 offset, representing the climate impact of the tree planting initiative.

nodes.label: Mangrove Tree Ecosystem
nodes.description: Close-up photograph of mangrove trees with characteristic arching prop roots in reddish-brown soil, green leafy canopy against a bright sky. Depicts a coastal tropical mangrove habitat used in reforestation initiatives.

nodes.label: Coastal Mangrove Reforestation Site
nodes.description: Natural habitat photo illustrating active or target reforestation site. Mangroves provide coastal protection, carbon sequestration, and marine biodiversity support.

nodes.label: Sustainable Technology Week Proclamation
nodes.description: Official proclamation from the Office of the Mayor, City of Vancouver, British Columbia, proclaiming 'Sustainable Technology Week' the week of October 20â€“27, 2025. Signed by Mayor Ken Sim. Recognizes Oasis of Change Inc. as a youth-founded nonprofit that planted 9,000+ trees in 30+ countries and prevented 7,000 plastic bottles from entering the ocean. Promotes sustainable web design, efficient hosting, and mindful device use.

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Civic Recognition for Sustainability Leadership
nodes.description: Proclamation specifically names Oasis of Change Inc. as demonstrating 'global leadership in climate action' and advancing sustainability locally through energy-efficient websites, education on responsible technology use, and partnerships with nonprofits and businesses.

nodes.label: Tree-Nation Partnership Logo
nodes.description: Partnership badge stating 'In Collaboration With tree-nation'. Features the Tree-Nation logo: a stylized tree with circular nodes on branches enclosed in a teal-to-green gradient circle. The wordmark 'tree-nation' is in a teal-to-green gradient font.

nodes.label: Oasis of Change and Tree-Nation Collaboration
nodes.description: Visual representation of the formal reforestation collaboration between Oasis of Change Inc. and Tree-Nation, a global tree-planting platform. Tree-Nation facilitates verified tree planting in multiple countries.

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Icon Mark (SVG)
nodes.description: Standalone icon/emblem for Oasis of Change. Square-format SVG (860x900px viewBox). Complex organic mark featuring nature-inspired imagery â€” likely a tree, leaf, or circular growth motif â€” rendered with multi-stop linear gradients. Dominant palette: forest green (#2f8b3b), bright green (#3bb63e), with gradient transitions across the ~23â€“26% red, 67â€“70% green, 29â€“33% blue range. Accent colors include deep blue (#3246a1) and sky blue (#4493d6), suggesting water or sky elements consistent with 'oasis' imagery. Highly detailed vector artwork with multiple clip paths and gradient definitions.
nodes.color_palette_description: Forest greens (primary), bright emerald greens, deep blue and sky blue accents

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Official Wordmark (SVG)
nodes.description: Full horizontal wordmark for Oasis of Change. Wide-format SVG (6000x1194px, viewBox 4500x895). Combines the icon mark in the left portion (~0â€“900px range) with the full brand name 'OASIS OF CHANGE' as 15 white-filled letter path groups (transform positions 1059â€“3755) against a colored background band. Typography rendered as outlined paths â€” all-caps, wide-set display lettering. Colors: white letterforms on colored ground; green tones (#2f8b3b, #3bb63e) in icon section; blue (#3246a1) for organic connecting element; dark gray (#545454) for secondary elements; sky blue (#4493d6) accent. The wordmark is fully vectorised with no embedded fonts.
nodes.color_palette_description: White letterforms on green/blue colored band; forest green icon; dark gray and blue accents
nodes.typography_style: All-caps display sans/slab, wide tracking, bold weight, rendered as outlined paths

nodes.label: Web Ready Logo Badge (WebP)
nodes.description: Square web-optimized badge (~500x500px). Deep forest green background with white 'Web Ready' text in a rounded serif / slab-serif typeface â€” warm, friendly, and organic in character. A small white seedling/sprout icon (two leaves on a stem) sits above the letter 'y' in 'Ready', reinforcing the growth and nature theme. The typography has a hand-crafted, approachable quality consistent with the Oasis of Change brand ethos. Used as a program or product sub-brand badge.
nodes.color_palette_description: Deep forest green background (~#1a6b2b or similar), pure white text and icon
nodes.typography_style: Rounded slab-serif, mixed case, bold weight, approachable and organic feel
nodes.icon_element: White seedling/sprout (two leaves on stem) above letter 'y'

nodes.label: Brand Color: Forest Green (#2f8b3b)
nodes.description: Primary brand green â€” deep forest green used in both the icon mark and full wordmark. Hex #2f8b3b. Represents nature, growth, sustainability.

nodes.label: Brand Color: Bright Green (#3bb63e)
nodes.description: Secondary brand green â€” bright emerald green used as accent/highlight in the icon mark and wordmark. Hex #3bb63e. Used for lighter areas in gradient transitions, suggesting vitality and growth.

nodes.label: Brand Color: Deep Blue (#3246a1)
nodes.description: Blue accent â€” deep indigo-blue used in both icon mark and official wordmark. Hex #3246a1. Likely represents water (oasis theme) or sky. Appears in organic path elements.

nodes.label: Brand Color: Sky Blue (#4493d6)
nodes.description: Light sky blue accent. Hex #4493d6. Used in gradient sections of icon and wordmark, suggesting open sky, hope, or water.

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Brand Identity
nodes.description: Overarching brand identity for Oasis of Change. Visual language is nature-forward, growth-oriented, and earthy: forest greens as dominant palette, blue water/sky accents, white clean lettering. Icon combines organic natural forms (trees, leaves, sprouts) with the concept of transformation. Typography is bold, approachable, and all-caps in the primary wordmark, with a warmer slab-serif in sub-brand badges. The brand communicates environmental stewardship, personal and community transformation, and a connection to nature.

nodes.label: Brand Motif: Seedling / Growth Icon
nodes.description: Recurring brand motif: seedling with two leaves on a stem. Visible explicitly in the web-ready-logo.webp as a white sprout icon. This growth motif is consistent with the organic nature of the icon mark paths and the brand name 'Oasis of Change'. Represents new beginnings, nurturing, environmental growth, and personal transformation.

nodes.label: Brand Typography: Display All-Caps Sans/Slab
nodes.description: Primary brand typography style for official wordmark: all-caps, bold, wide-tracked display letterforms rendered as outlined SVG paths. The letterforms suggest a geometric or humanist sans-serif or slab-serif typeface â€” clean, impactful, and confident. The sub-brand (Web Ready badge) uses a warmer, more rounded slab-serif, indicating typographic flexibility within the brand system.

nodes.label: Stanley Park Ecology Society
nodes.description: Logo featuring bold black sans-serif text reading 'STANLEY PARK' above 'ECOLOGY SOCIETY', with a large stylized green sphere (globe/earth motif) overlapping the word 'ECOLOGY'. The sphere has a shiny highlight suggesting a 3D globe. Colors: deep black text, medium green sphere. Organization type: environmental/ecology non-profit focused on urban park ecology, likely based in Vancouver, BC (Stanley Park).

nodes.label: Sustainable WWW
nodes.description: Logo with a circular icon on the left containing two leaf/plant silhouettes arranged to suggest growth or renewal inside a thin circle. To the right, 'SUSTAINABLE' in small caps above 'WWW' in large bold text. Entire logo is in dark charcoal/near-black. Organization type: sustainable web/technology initiative focused on environmental impact of the internet.

nodes.label: West End Seniors' Network
nodes.description: Logo with a circular icon on the left showing three stylized human figures arranged in a pinwheel/circular pattern suggesting community and connection. Figures are in teal/dark cyan and yellow/gold. To the right, 'WEST END' in yellow/gold bold text, 'SENIORS'' in teal bold text, and 'NETWORK' in dark green bold text. Organization type: community social services network serving seniors in the West End neighbourhood (likely Vancouver, BC).

nodes.label: Oasis of Change Partner Organizations
nodes.description: Collective concept node representing the partner organizations displayed on the Oasis of Change website. Partners span ecology, sustainable technology, and community social services sectors.

links.detail: Loads Google Translate script and uses google.translate.TranslateElement API
After applying dictionary translations, elements are marked with notranslate class to prevent GT from double-translating
Uses localStorage (ooc-lang) and googtrans cookie for language persistence across page loads
i18n injects switcher into nav structure built by site.js, dispatches resize event to trigger nav overflow recheck
Provides trilingual support (EN/FR/ES) for the Oasis of Change website
initMobileNav() contains the three-stage overflow measurement and collapse logic
initImageSlider() creates pointer-drag before/after comparison widgets
logCuriousCoderMessage() prints ASCII logo and sustainability message to console
prefersReducedMotion() check gates all nav panel animations
Allowlist comments explicitly state classes are injected via site.js (nav dropdown, impact stats, ticker, footer)
Provides core interactive behaviors (nav, accordion, slider) for the website
extract_css_classes() parses public/css/tailwind/tailwind.min.css
CUSTOM_CLASSES set defines ~35 JS-injected class names excluded from missing-class reports
Audits the website CSS to detect visually broken classes and unused CSS bloat
Instructs to read graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md before answering architecture questions
Graph report documents MST Website Before/After Case Study Comparison hyperedge; image compare widget provides the UI for such comparisons
Sitemap directive points to https://oasisofchange.com/sitemap.xml
Allows all user-agents to crawl all paths of the website
Explains GT role as fallback: handles ALL remaining page content that the dictionary does not cover
injectGTHideCSS() hides GT default chrome to maintain clean design while leveraging GT translation
Dictionary handles high-visibility nav/footer strings instantly; GT handles the long tail of page content
Explains why curated dictionary is applied before Google Translate: instant rendering, no flash of untranslated content
Code comments explain the three-stage approach: Stage 1 full logo, Stage 2 compact logo to free space, Stage 3 hamburger collapse
ASCII logo and conscious coder messaging reinforces Oasis of Change sustainability brand identity to developers
Comments explain these are expected - styled elsewhere, preventing false positives in the audit

links.note: The hero-bg photo is visibly used as the blurred background image in the after-redesign hero section
Same brand/organization (Mittler Senior Technology); hero photo connects to both design states
Direct before/after pair documenting the same website pre- and post-redesign
Both are case study assets in the same portfolio; both use full-bleed photography as hero backgrounds for mission-driven organizations
Icon mark is embedded as the left portion of the full wordmark SVG; same clip path IDs (e.g. #48c7d8a31b) appear in both files
Same forest green palette, same seedling motif, same approachable brand tone as main brand assets
Seedling icon explicitly visible in web-ready-logo.webp
Both are green palette variants used together in gradient transitions within the same logo
Both blue tones used in same logo assets â€” likely represent water/sky (oasis theme)

hyperedges.label: Mittler Senior Technology Case Study Asset Group
hyperedges.note: All three image files plus derived comparison concept together constitute the Mittler Senior Technology website redesign case study
